full_name: Stochastic Subgradient Method (Convex, Constant Step Size)
archetype_note: Novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems. No gradF field (non-smooth objective). Variance bound derived internally from uniform oracle bound (Pattern I).
reference: Lan, First-order and Stochastic Optimization Methods for Machine Learning, Chapter 4 (Theorem 4.1 variant)

space.description: Hilbert space E over the reals

sample_space.description: Sample space S (for the stochastic oracle) and probability space Ω

objective.description: f : E → ℝ, convex. Convexity is expressed in primitive subgradient form — no abstract subdifferential symbol.
objective.lean_hypothesis: hsubgrad : ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ
objective.note: This replaces the standard condition g ∈ ∂f(w) because Mathlib has no subdifferential type. The inequality directly encodes subgradient membership.

oracle.description: Stochastic first-order oracle gradL : E → S → E
lean_hypotheses: hbounded : ∀ w s, ‖gradL w s‖ ≤ G
oracle.note: G is the uniform bound on oracle outputs. There is no unbiasedness hypothesis — the subgradient condition subsumes it.

noise.description: i.i.d. random samples ξ t : Ω → S
lean_hypotheses: hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P

integrability.description: Required integrability conditions (passed as hypotheses, not derived)
lean_hypotheses: h_int_f : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω)) setup.P
h_int_norm_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P
h_int_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P

update_rule.lean: setup.process (t+1) ω = setup.process t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)
update_rule.lean_definition: SubgradientSetup.process := sgdProcess setup.w₀ setup.η setup.gradL setup.ξ
update_rule.note: process reuses sgdProcess from SGD verbatim (Archetype B pattern — reuse recursion, change proof structure).

3_key_lemma_chain.title: Key Lemma Chain
3_key_lemma_chain.overview: Three proof steps: (A) per-step integrated bound, (B) telescoping sum, (C) algebraic rearrangement.

step_A.title: STEP A — Per-step integrated bound
step_A.lean_have: hstep : ∀ t < T, ∫ ω, ‖w_{t+1}(ω) - w*‖² ∂P ≤ ∫ ω, ‖w_t(ω) - w*‖² ∂P - 2η(E[f(w_t)] - f(w*)) + η²G²

sub_lemmas.title: Inner product lower bound (Pattern H)
sub_lemmas.direction_note: hsubgrad gives ⟨g, w* - w_t⟩; must flip to ⟨g, w_t - w*⟩. Use inner_neg_right after rewriting w* - w_t = -(w_t - w*).
sub_lemmas.lean_tactic: rw [show wStar - setup.process t ω = -(setup.process t ω - wStar) by abel, inner_neg_right] at h_sub; linarith

sub_lemmas.title: Variance bound (Pattern I)
sub_lemmas.lean_tactic: have h_var_bound := hasBoundedVariance_of_pointwise_bound hbounded h_wt_meas (setup.hξ_meas t) setup.hgL h_int_sq_t

step_C.title: STEP C — Final algebraic rearrangement
step_C.goal: Divide both sides of STEP B by 2ηT, then simplify to the standard bound form.

4_final_theorem.title: Final Convergence Theorem
4_final_theorem.lean_statement: (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2

interpretation.first_term: ‖w₀ - w*‖² / (2ηT) — initial distance term, vanishes as T → ∞
interpretation.second_term: ηG²/2 — variance floor from oracle noise, minimized by small η
interpretation.optimal_T: Set η = ‖w₀ - w*‖ / (G√T) to balance the two terms, giving O(1/√T) rate
